successCriteria.description: src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditRecord with fields id, entityType, entityId, and action, plus CreateAuditRecordInput with entityType, entityId, and action.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports AuditRepository with create and findByEntity methods using AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput types from audit.model.ts.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exports PostgreSqlAuditRepository as an abstract class implementing the AuditRepository contract with matching method signatures.
Repository contracts use AuditRecord types imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts and introduce no circular dependencies.
tests/unit/modules/audit/audit.repository.spec.ts exists, uses Vitest, and verifies audit contract exports and TypeScript type compatibility.
The canonical audit_records schema is represented exactly as specified: id UUID primary key, entity_type VARCHAR(100) not null, entity_id UUID not null, action VARCHAR(100) not null.

constraints: Keep implementation limited to audit persistence contracts and related type definitions/tests.
Create only the audit module files described (approximately 3-5 files).
Repository pattern: all database access must be represented through repository interfaces/contracts; no direct database access implementations in this phase.
Audit support must remain isolated and reusable by later workflow services.
Use the exact interface and method signatures provided by the architecture specification.
All repository method signatures must use AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts.
No circular dependencies between audit model and repository contracts.
Audit records are a platform-wide requirement; this phase defines audit persistence contracts only, not audit-writing behavior.
Input validation, RBAC enforcement, async error handling, and sensitive-data logging concerns are not to be implemented here because no API or service behavior is introduced.
outOfScope: Leave approval or rejection workflow services.
Fastify routes, controllers, or API endpoints.
Database repository implementations or query logic.
Notification domain models, repositories, or services.
Any changes outside the audit module contracts, related test file, and schema definition required by this phase.
